content:
# Matthew Chapter 25 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_025_page_001.jpg, MAT_025_page_002.jpg

1. Then the kingdom of the heavens will be likened to ten virgins who, having taken the lamps of them, went out to meet the bridegroom.

2. And five of them were foolish and five [were] wise.

3. For the foolish, having taken the lamps of them, did not take with them oil.

4. But the wise took oil in the vessels with the lamps of them.

5. And the bridegroom delaying, they all became drowsy and were sleeping.

6. And in [the] middle of [the] night a cry has come, 'Behold, the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.'

7. Then all those virgins arose and trimmed the lamps of them.

8. And the foolish said to the wise, 'Give us from the oil of you, for the lamps of us are going out.'

9. But the wise answered, saying, 'Lest there may not be enough for us and you, go rather to the [ones] selling and buy for yourselves.'

10. And [while] they were going away to buy, the bridegroom came, and the [ones] ready went in with him to the wedding-feast, and the door was shut.

11. And afterward come also the remaining virgins, saying, 'Lord, lord, open to us.'

12. But answering, he said, 'Truly I say to you, I do not know you.'

13. Watch therefore, for you do not know the day nor the hour.

14. For [it is] just as a man going on a journey called the slaves of him and delivered to them the possessions of him.

15. And to one he gave five talents, and to another two, and to another one — to each according to the own ability of him — and went on his journey.

16. Going, the [one] having received the five talents worked with them and gained another five.

17. Likewise the [one] [having received] the two gained another two.

18. But the [one] having received the one, going away, dug in the earth and hid the silver of the lord of him.

19. And after much time the lord of those slaves comes and settles account with them.

20. And coming, the [one] having received the five talents brought another five talents, saying, 'Lord, five talents you delivered to me; behold, another five talents I have gained.'

21. The lord of him said to him, 'Well [done], good and faithful slave. Over a few [things] you were faithful; over many [things] I will set you. Enter into the joy of the lord of you.'

22. And coming also the [one] [having received] the two talents said, 'Lord, two talents you delivered to me; behold, another two talents I have gained.'

23. The lord of him said to him, 'Well [done], good and faithful slave. Over a few [things] you were faithful; over many [things] I will set you. Enter into the joy of the lord of you.'

24. And coming also the [one] having received the one talent said, 'Lord, I knew you that you are a hard man, reaping where you did not sow and gathering from where you did not scatter.

25. And being afraid, going away, I hid the talent of you in the earth. Behold, you have what is yours.'

26. And answering, the lord of him said to him, 'Wicked and lazy slave! You knew that I reap where I did not sow and gather from where I did not scatter?

27. It was necessary therefore [for] you to put the silver of me with the bankers, and coming I would have received what is mine with interest.

28. Take therefore from him the talent and give [it] to the [one] having the ten talents.

29. For to everyone having, it will be given, and he will have abundance. But from the [one] not having, even what he has will be taken from him.

30. And the useless slave cast out into the outer darkness; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.'

31. And when the Son of the Man comes in the glory of him, and all the angels with him, then he will sit upon [the] throne of glory of him.

32. And will be gathered before him all the nations, and he will separate them from one another, just as the shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

33. And he will set the sheep on [the] right of him, but the goats on [the] left.

34. Then the king will say to the [ones] on [the] right of him, 'Come, the [ones] blessed of the Father of me, inherit the kingdom having been prepared for you from [the] foundation of [the] world.

35. For I was hungry and you gave me to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me to drink, I was a stranger and you took me in,

36. naked and you clothed me, I was sick and you visited me, I was in prison and you came to me.'

37. Then the righteous will answer him, saying, 'Lord, when did we see you hungry and fed [you], or thirsty and gave [you] drink?

38. And when did we see you a stranger and took [you] in, or naked and clothed [you]?

39. And when did we see you sick or in prison and came to you?'

40. And answering, the king will say to them, 'Truly I say to you, inasmuch as you did [it] to one of these the least of the brothers of me, you did [it] to me.'

41. Then he will say also to the [ones] on [the] left, 'Go from me, [you] cursed, into the eternal fire having been prepared for the devil and the angels of him.

42. For I was hungry and you did not give me to eat, I was thirsty and you did not give me to drink,

43. I was a stranger and you did not take me in, naked and you did not clothe me, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.'

44. Then they also will answer, saying, 'Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and did not serve you?'

45. Then he will answer them, saying, 'Truly I say to you, inasmuch as you did not do [it] to one of these the least, neither did you do [it] to me.'

46. And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **τάλαντα (vv.15-28):** A talent was a unit of weight (about 75 pounds / 34 kg), not a coin. One talent of silver was equivalent to roughly 6,000 denarii — about 20 years' wages for a laborer. Five talents was an enormous sum. The English word "talent" (meaning skill) derives from this parable.

2. **v.13 — "the day nor the hour":** Sinaiticus reads simply ἡμέραν οὐδὲ τὴν ὥραν ("the day nor the hour"), without the later addition "in which the Son of Man comes" found in some manuscripts.

3. **v.31 — "all the angels":** Sinaiticus reads πάντες οἱ ἄγγελοι, confirming "all the angels" (some manuscripts add "holy" — ἅγιοι). The Sinaiticus reading is shorter.

4. **v.40 — "the least of these my brothers":** ἑνὶ τούτων τῶν ἀδελφῶν μου τῶν ἐλαχίστων — "brothers" (ἀδελφῶν) is debated: does Jesus mean all suffering people, or specifically his disciples? The literal Greek leaves this ambiguous.

5. **v.46 — κόλασιν αἰώνιον / ζωὴν αἰώνιον:** "Eternal punishment" and "eternal life" share the same adjective αἰώνιον. Whatever duration "eternal life" has, "eternal punishment" has the same. The symmetry is precise and deliberate.

— EMET, AI Translator
